subject: Tiếng Anh 12
name: Part 4: Reading
description: Đọc hiểu về các loài có nguy cơ tuyệt chủng

sections.heading: Reading Skills
sections.content:
Chủ đề: Endangered species and conservation efforts

Kỹ năng đọc hiểu:

  • Skimming: Đọc lướt để nắm ý chính
  • Scanning: Tìm thông tin cụ thể
  • Guessing meaning from context: Đoán nghĩa từ ngữ cảnh
  • Identifying main ideas: Xác định ý chính
  • Understanding references: Hiểu từ thay thế

exercises.passage:
Wildlife Conservation: A Global Challenge

Wildlife conservation has become more urgent than ever before. Many species are disappearing faster than scientists can study them. If we don't take immediate action, we will lose countless species forever.

The main threats to wildlife are habitat loss, poaching, and climate change. Deforestation destroys the homes of millions of animals. The more forests we cut down, the fewer places animals have to live. Tigers, for example, are more endangered now than they were 50 years ago because their forest habitats have shrunk dramatically. Unless we stop destroying habitats, many species will become extinct within our lifetime.

Poaching is another serious problem. Illegal hunters kill elephants for ivory, rhinos for horns, and tigers for their skins. The demand for wildlife products is as high as it has ever been. If governments enforced stricter laws and punished poachers more severely, fewer animals would be killed.

Climate change affects wildlife in ways that are not always obvious. As temperatures rise, animals must adapt or move to cooler areas. However, many species cannot move fast enough. The faster the climate changes, the harder it is for species to survive.

Despite these challenges, there are success stories. Giant pandas, once critically endangered, have recovered thanks to intensive conservation efforts in China. This shows that if we invest in conservation and protect habitats, species can recover. As long as we work together, there is hope for wildlife.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Wildlife conservation is urgent and requires action
questions.explanation: Đoạn văn nói về sự cấp thiết của bảo tồn động vật hoang dã.

questions.question: What is the main cause of habitat loss?
questions.explanation: Phá rừng là nguyên nhân chính gây mất môi trường sống.

questions.question: What conservation success is mentioned?
questions.explanation: Gấu trúc khổng lồ là ví dụ thành công.

questions.question: How can we help conservation?
questions.explanation: Chúng ta có thể giúp bằng cách đầu tư và bảo vệ môi trường sống.

exercises.question: What does 'endangered' mean?
exercises.explanation: Endangered = at risk of extinction (có nguy cơ tuyệt chủng).

exercises.question: What is 'poaching'?
exercises.explanation: Poaching là săn bắt trái phép.

exercises.question: What is a 'habitat'?
exercises.explanation: Habitat là môi trường sống tự nhiên.

exercises.question: According to the passage, wildlife conservation is:
exercises.explanation: Bảo tồn động vật hoang dã là cấp thiết và cần thiết.

exercises.question: What should we do to save species?
exercises.explanation: Chúng ta nên bảo vệ môi trường sống và thực thi luật.

exercises.question_text: Based on the passage, decide True or False:
exercises.explanation: a) True - Mất môi trường sống đe dọa động vật. b) False - Nhiều loài có nguy cơ. c) True - Săn bắt trái phép nghiêm trọng. d) False - Bảo tồn có thể thành công.

options: Tigers are more endangered than 50 years ago.
exercises.explanation: a) True - Hổ có nguy cơ hơn. b) False - Biến đổi khí hậu ảnh hưởng. c) True - Gấu trúc đã phục hồi. d) False - Không nên phá hủy.

exercises.question_text: Complete: Animals at risk of extinction are _____. (one word)
exercises.explanation: Endangered - có nguy cơ tuyệt chủng.

exercises.question_text: What is the Vietnamese meaning of 'poaching'?
exercises.explanation: Poaching nghĩa là săn bắt trái phép.

exercises.question_text: Complete: The natural living environment is called _____. (one word)
exercises.explanation: Habitat - môi trường sống tự nhiên.
